ddls:
CREATE TABLE "energy_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the data was recorded */,
  "energy_type_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the energy type */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the region */,
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the data source */,
  "production_value" REAL /* Production value for the given year, energy type, and region */,
  "consumption_value" REAL /* Consumption value for the given year, energy type, and region */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement (e.g., barrels, cubic feet, kilowatt-hours) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time of the data */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional context or notes about the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_energy_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("energy_type_id") REFERENCES energy_types ("energy_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("data_source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_energy_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "energy_types" (
  "energy_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each energy type */,
  "energy_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the energy type (e.g., oil, natural gas, coal, renewable) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the energy type */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Default unit of measurement for the energy type */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* Link to the source of information about the energy type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("energy_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., Bakken, Permian, Marcellus) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the region is located */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographical latitude of the region */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographical longitude of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "data_source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "data_source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., EIA, API, company reports) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* Link to the data source */,
  "reliability_rating" INTEGER /* Reliability rating of the data source (1-5) */,
  "last_checked" TEXT /* Last time the data source was checked for updates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_source_id")
)
